preamble: Nasal Raising describes the movement of a nasalized vowel to a higher position in the vowel space. While lowering is more common for nasal vowels, raising occurs sporadically or in specific dialectal variations.

examples.note: Dialectal variation in Brazilian Portuguese where word-final [ɐ̃] raises to [ĩ].
Sporadic raising and closure of the nasal vowel in the diphthong.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
